reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m completely confident my own answer is correct (11 × 8 = 88), and in all previous games every puzzle has been simple arithmetic with no conflicting capsules—whenever anyone checked, they agreed. That makes the chance that any of Q2–Q4 is wrong much smaller than the 1-in-4 risk of dying from random elimination if, by bad luck, nobody volunteers. Verifying would cost a coin and barely reduce an already tiny error risk, while still leaving the random-death risk if no one steps up. Volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my survival chances and preserves all 4 coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All puzzles so far have been simple arithmetic, and across all previous games every initial capsule has been correct; the only death we’ve seen was from random elimination when nobody volunteered. My own puzzle (12 × 7) is trivial, so I’m highly confident my capsule is right, and it’s very unlikely that A, C, or D missed theirs given the same pattern. Passing keeps some chance that everyone hesitates and we get another random death, and verifying would just burn a coin without meaningfully improving already very high confidence in the password. Volunteering now with each player’s own capsule (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) best avoids random-elimination risk while keeping my full 4 coins with overwhelming probability.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All puzzles so far have been simple arithmetic, with no conflicting capsules and no incorrect passwords once everyone’s initial answers were in. My own puzzle (77 - 38) is trivial and I’m confident my sealed answer is correct, and past games strongly suggest the others are reliable too. Verifying would cost 1 coin and only marginally reduce risk, since the other questions would still be unverified and there has been no sign of errors. Passing or verifying also keeps the risk that nobody volunteers and a random player dies. Given the very low chance the password is wrong and the higher cost of hesitation, I’ll volunteer immediately with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
